channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Delhi court grants bail to senior citizen accused in rape case citing inconsistencies and victim’s withdrawal
item.description: A Delhi court has granted regular bail to a 63-year-old man accused of rape, observing multiple inconsistencies in the prosecution’s case and noting the complainant’s decision to withdraw from the pro...

item.title: Iran insists no talks held with US despite Trump's claim of productive discussions
item.description: Iran has strongly rejected claims by US President Donald Trump that Washington and Tehran have been in contact over recent days to discuss ending the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Senior Irania...

item.title: Veteran Air Traffic Controller Reflects on Air Canada Jet Crash at LaGuardia
item.description: An Air Canada jet recently crashed at New York’s LaGuardia Airport, prompting passengers to help each other escape the wreckage. Eyewitnesses and experts, including a longtime Canadian air traffic con...

item.title: How Organisations Can Adapt Workplace Culture for Gen Z Employees
item.description: South Africa’s workforce is seeing a generational shift, with Generation Z, born from the late 1990s to early 2010s, gradually becoming a significant part of the labour market. Contrary to common ster...

item.title: Former High Court Judge Julie Manning Remembered for Integrity and Humble Life
item.description: As Tanzania prepares to lay to rest retired High Court Judge Julie Catherine Manning, many people across the country continue to give tributes, describing her as a person of strong integrity, humility...

item.title: Sule Says Governors Give Tinubu Straight Talk About Nigeria's Problems
item.description: Nasarawa State Governor, Abdullahi Sule, don yan say the state governors dey frank and dey tell President Bola Tinubu true matter about how things dey for Nigeria. Na during interview with Channels Te...

item.title: Progress on the Freedom of Information Bill in the Philippines under Marcos Jr.
item.description: The Philippines is moving closer to passing a Freedom of Information (FOI) law after more than three decades of stalled attempts. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. included an FOI bill in his legislative...

item.title: IGP Olutunji Disu Approves Nationwide Redeployment of Police Commissioners, Tijani Fatai Posted to Lagos Command
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police, Olutunji Disu, has approved a fresh round of postings for several Commissioners of Police across different state commands and formations in Nigeria. The directive, con...

item.title: PS Omollo Encourages Nyanza People to Foster Unity and Peace Regardless of Politics
item.description: Interior Principal Secretary Raymond Omollo has appealed to the residents of Nyanza region to live peacefully and embrace tolerance despite their political differences. Speaking during an event in Hom...

item.title: Sir Lindsay Hoyle calls for extradition of convicted fraudster Pamela Gwinnett after pensioner scam
item.description: Commons Speaker Sir Lindsay Hoyle has called for immediate justice for the family of Joan Green, an 89-year-old pensioner from Chorley, Lancashire, who was defrauded of nearly £300,000 by Pamela Gwinn...

item.title: Experts flag potential insider trading in US-Iran ceasefire bets on Polymarket
item.description: Several newly created accounts on the online prediction platform Polymarket have placed bets totalling nearly $70,000 on a US-Iran ceasefire occurring before 31 March. Experts suggest these bets may r...

item.title: Wayne Atkinson: Yorta Yorta Elder on Activism, Land Justice and Self-Determination
item.description: Beyond the Meeting of the Waters tells the life story of Yorta Yorta Elder Wayne Atkinson, co-authored with Catherine Guinness, highlighting his decades-long activism and commitment to Aboriginal just...

item.title: Parents detain education official over long-standing school safety concerns
item.description: A tense situation unfolded at Duma Primary School in Pienaar, Mpumalanga, on Monday when parents and the school governing body (SGB) detained the circuit manager in the staff room. The protest was aim...

item.title: Four labourers killed, 14 injured in cold storage building collapse in Prayagraj, ammonia gas leak reported
item.description: In a tragic incident on Monday afternoon in Chandapur village of Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh, a portion of a cold storage facility suddenly collapsed, resulting in the death of four labourers and injurie...

item.title: OFWs Repatriated Amid Ongoing Tensions in the Middle East
item.description: The GMA Integrated News report dated March 24, 2026, provides real-time updates on the ongoing conflict in the Middle East and its impact on Filipinos working in the region. According to the Departmen...

item.title: COMESA Urges Businesses to Avoid Price Hikes Despite Global Supply Challenges
item.description: The COMESA Competition and Consumer Commission (CCCC) has issued a warning to all businesses operating in its member states not to engage in unfair pricing or anti-competitive practices, even as globa...

item.title: Varanasi court refuses bail to 14 men facing charges related to Ganga iftar party
item.description: In Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, a local court on Monday denied bail to 14 Muslim men who were arrested after hosting an iftar party on a boat in the river Ganga, during which they allegedly ate chicken bi...

item.title: NFF approves statute reforms and fixes September date for next leadership election
item.description: The Nigeria Football Federation (NFF) don make some major decisions wey go shape the future of football administration for the country. During the NFF Extraordinary General Assembly wey hold for Yenag...

item.title: Swish secures $38 million Series B to expand its fast food delivery model in India
item.description: Bengaluru-based food delivery startup Swish has raised $38 million in a Series B round led by Hara Global and Bain Capital Ventures, with participation from Accel, Stride Ventures, and Alteria Capital...

item.title: The Urgent Need for an Updated Anti-Espionage Law in the Philippines
item.description: The article argues that the Philippines urgently needs a modern anti-espionage law to address evolving security threats. It cites the 2024 raid in Bamban, Tarlac, where nearly 1,000 workers, including...
